headline: IT Helpdesk Support Officer | Troubleshooting | Secure Systems
summary: Computer Science graduate with hands-on software support, incident workflow, technical documentation, networking, and secure systems project experience. Built support-oriented tools covering incident triage, lifecycle tracking, root-cause capture, secure deployment, and vulnerability remediation, with a practical focus on clear communication, reliable troubleshooting, and careful escalation of technical issues.

education.details: Major in Software Engineering and Cybersecurity
Cybersecurity specialization; relevant coursework/projects include software testing and analysis, machine learning/AI, secure systems, and applied security review work.

bullets.text: Designed and developed a full-stack AI-assisted resume tailoring platform that generates structured HTML and PDF outputs from job descriptions using Anthropic Claude and OpenAI APIs with dual-provider fallback logic.
Implemented strict no-fabrication validation logic and a structured JSON resume format with weighted bullet points and importance scores to enforce factual consistency across generated outputs.
Extended the platform into a multi-user SaaS with Supabase Auth, server-side Flask sessions, and a per-user generation dashboard tracking usage history.
Implemented monthly usage limits enforced server-side — blocking generation requests at 10/month per user without hitting the AI API, with usage tracked in Supabase with RLS-enforced data isolation.
Built a GitHub Actions CI/CD security pipeline with Bandit, Semgrep, Gitleaks, and pip-audit — scanning for vulnerabilities, secrets, and CVEs on every push to main.
Identified and resolved a medium severity Bandit finding (B104 interface binding vulnerability) surfaced by the pipeline, documenting the fix in the project README.
Deployed the application with VPN-only access (Tailscale) on a cloud VM to restrict public exposure and protect user data.
Maintained structured project documentation and validation checks so generated outputs stay consistent, traceable, and easy to review before use.
Built and maintained a professional portfolio website to present software engineering, cybersecurity, AI, and DevSecOps projects with clear project narratives, links, and technical positioning.
Implemented a responsive, polished front-end using modern JavaScript tooling and a reusable visual system to keep project showcases consistent across desktop and mobile.
Completed a website and local repository security review, then marked the project secured across public-facing and local scopes with Sentinel artefacts retained for audit/reference.

Expanded the portfolio into a central project evidence hub with security, AI, DevSecOps, and support case studies, including safe interactive demos and documented local-run paths where public hosting would be risky or unnecessary.
Built a conversion-focused React/TypeScript landing page for SaaS-focused penetration testing and security readiness services, positioning technical security testing for founders and product teams.
Created a practical outreach funnel with SaaS founder messaging, service positioning, and LinkedIn DM templates to turn security testing capability into a client acquisition workflow.
Completed and documented security review coverage for both the public-facing website and local repository, with Sentinel artefacts linked for evidence and follow-up tracking.
Documented review outcomes and follow-up items in a repeatable security review workflow, keeping evidence available for later audit and remediation tracking.
Built and launched a cybersecurity assessment platform aligned to the ACSC Essential Eight maturity model, deployed on Vercel with Supabase for data persistence.
Designed a 19-question structured assessment with automated scoring, category-level risk breakdowns, and CTI-backed findings.
Automated HTML report delivery using SendGrid transactional email and integrated Calendly booking to convert assessments into consultations.
Deployed a live marketing funnel with a landing page and LinkedIn launch assets to drive inbound leads.
Integrated a live web vulnerability scanner into the results page — triggered after the Essential Eight assessment, with findings stored in Supabase and included in the HTML email report.
Implemented an email gate for new users prior to scanning, with direct scan access for returning authenticated users.

Developed a full-stack incident management console with a React/TypeScript frontend and FastAPI/Python backend, containerised with Docker and preserved as a portfolio case study/local-run project after public Render hosting was archived.
Implemented structured incident lifecycle tracking with severity classification, status transitions, ownership, SLA breach indicators, and root-cause analysis capture.

Designed the console around support-team workflows including ticket ownership, severity triage, status updates, SLA indicators, and root-cause notes to make issue handling easier to track and escalate.
Built a full-stack GitHub Actions security pipeline covering Python (Bandit, pip-audit) and JavaScript (npm audit, Gitleaks) with a consolidated fail-gate enforcing zero HIGH findings before merge.

Discovered 8 CVEs in backend Python dependencies via pip-audit; replaced python-jose (no upstream fix path) with PyJWT and migrated the JWT authentication implementation to eliminate the vulnerability chain.
Resolved a high severity frontend vulnerability identified by npm audit by upgrading the Vite toolchain to v7 and associated plugin dependencies.

Pivoted the project presentation from an always-on hosted service to a safer portfolio simulation/case study, keeping the operational workflow visible while avoiding unnecessary public infrastructure exposure.
Built a full-stack web vulnerability scanner with 6 scanner modules covering security headers, SSL/TLS, sensitive path exposure, cookie flags, open redirects, and information disclosure.
Implemented concurrent URL scanning via ThreadPoolExecutor — each URL scanned in parallel with a shared requests.Session for efficiency, supporting single and batch scan modes.
Built a React/Tailwind dashboard with severity-coded findings (CRITICAL/HIGH/MEDIUM/LOW/INFO), collapsible per-URL sections, and client-side JSON report export.
Added a GitHub Actions CI/CD security pipeline with Bandit SAST, Gitleaks secrets detection, and pip-audit dependency scanning with fail-gate on HIGH findings.

Reworked the public presentation into a safe sample-results demo/case study instead of an unrestricted live scanner, with real arbitrary URL scanning deferred behind future auth, rate limits, and allowlist controls.
Built a full-stack security testing tool that probes LLMs for prompt injection, jailbreaking, system prompt extraction, data exfiltration, and role confusion attacks across 15 test cases.
Compared Llama 3.2 (local via Ollama) against Claude Haiku — Llama achieved 15/15 resistant while Haiku returned 3 FAIL and 5 PARTIAL, with HIGH severity prompt injection failures.
Built a React/Tailwind dashboard with per-category breakdowns, severity badges, expandable prompt/response rows, and JSON report export.
Designed an extensible JSON attack library so new test cases and categories can be added without modifying backend or frontend code.
Presented the tool through a safe interactive recorded-results demo in the portfolio, keeping real model/API execution protected for local or controlled environments.

Built an automated cybersecurity content pipeline that aggregates HIGH/CRITICAL CVEs from NVD, actively exploited vulnerabilities from CISA KEV, and AI security research from arXiv — then uses Claude Haiku to draft LinkedIn posts for review.

Implemented tone control (professional/conversational/technical) and a React/TypeScript dashboard for draft review and one-click clipboard copy, with portfolio presentation shifted to a safe interactive demo using sample threat-intel data.
Resolved 6 CVEs in dependencies (Flask, Flask-CORS, Requests) and a HIGH severity Bandit B201 finding (hardcoded Flask debug mode) surfaced by the CI/CD security pipeline.
Designed the workflow for scheduled Discord delivery through GitHub Actions/webhooks so generated cybersecurity briefings can be reviewed before posting publicly.
Benchmarked three small language models (Llama 3.2, Phi-3 Mini, Gemma 2) running entirely offline via Ollama on a CPU-only Ubuntu VM, with no GPU or cloud API dependency.
Designed a Python benchmarking script evaluating all 3 models across 4 task types (summarisation, JSON extraction, code generation, and multi-step reasoning) — 12 data points total.
Identified task-specific failure modes including two 120s timeouts that would not surface in quality-only evaluations, informing model selection recommendations by task type.
Documented speed vs quality tradeoffs: Gemma 2 fastest at 4.8 tok/s, Llama 3.2 most reliable (4/4 tasks), Phi-3 Mini best code quality but unreliable on structured output.
Added the benchmark as an interactive portfolio results explorer, making offline model tradeoffs easier to review without requiring a hosted backend or cloud inference.

projects.name: CSCI933 Machine Learning / Shakespeare RAG Coursework

bullets.text: Completed machine learning coursework comparing statistical regression models and neural network approaches on the Bike Sharing dataset, including data understanding, feature engineering, model evaluation, and report preparation.
Built and organized starter code for a Shakespeare small-language-model/RAG assignment, including chunking, retrieval, indexing, baseline generation, evaluation, and sample output workflows.

projects.name: CSCI926 Software Testing and Analysis Group Project

bullets.text: Completed a software testing and analysis group project covering verification and validation activities, test planning, reporting, and presentation of testing evidence for a real-world software project.
Studied and applied testing concepts including symbolic execution, test adequacy, functional testing, fuzzing, combinatorial testing, structural testing, and data-flow/control-flow models.
Built a cross-platform mobile application using React Native and TypeScript with a focus on privacy-conscious data handling for sensitive health-related user interactions.
Implemented Firebase integration for authentication, cloud data storage, and messaging workflows.
Added geolocation and media upload capabilities with structured in-app navigation.
Contributed to front-end and back-end feature development for a dynamic web platform.
Implemented responsive UI components and API-driven features to enhance usability and data interaction.
Collaborated with stakeholders to translate functional requirements into maintainable code.
Implemented custom front-end features for an eCommerce website with focus on clean layout and responsiveness.
Supported server-side functionality for product displays, transactional workflows, and content updates.
Enhanced mobile usability and cross-device consistency across the platform.

certifications: AWS Academy Graduate – AWS Academy Cloud Foundations award
target_roles: AI Security / Secure AI Tooling Graduate

graduate_cybersecurity: CSCI926 Software Testing and Analysis Group Project
